statement: For pilot-scale debugging, we need explicit cancel semantics plus a small set of bounded repair operations exposed to the agent: ensure_workflow_bound (start if missing), nudge_resume (resume if paused or re-trigger), and reproject_snapshot (force projection freshness). These should be first-class control actions rather than overloading a generic repair hook.
rationale: The current system lacks cancel and has a workflow-local repair hook stub, while Convex repair surfaces mix start/resume/nudge behavior implicitly. Pilot loops need explicit, auditable actions and consistent acknowledgements to avoid destructive resets as the only escape hatch.
keyAssumptions: Temporal cancellation is acceptable as the primary stop mechanism (activities may still need to be idempotent/compensating on retry)
A small bounded repair surface is sufficient for most recoverable stalls in pilot hardening
validationCriteria: Cancel marks the Temporal execution as canceled and the Convex domain row as canceled, and stops further attempt writes
Repair operations are idempotent and safe to run multiple times from an agent loop
Reproject_snapshot reduces false positive stale_projection stuck signals without changing execution
critiquePoints: Some failures may require more invasive repair (e.g., stage-local replay); bounded repair might be insufficient in rare cases
Cancel semantics can be tricky if a stage activity is mid-flight; may need cancellation-aware activity code
questions: Which actions should be Updates vs Signals vs direct client cancellation, given current Convex -> Temporal call boundary?
